findings.description: enqueue_job SCRAPE_BATCH path calls .single().execute() to fetch shop google_maps_url. If shop_id doesn't exist, .single() raises an unhandled APIError resulting in a 500 instead of a clean 404/422. Should wrap in try/except and return a proper HTTP error.
Dead letter queue test mock data references 'scrape_shop' job_type string which no longer exists in the JobType enum. Stale reference that could confuse future developers.
Test data uses placeholder IDs ('shop-1', 'sub-1', 'user-1') instead of realistic UUIDs per testing philosophy.
Test names framed around implementation details ('only_queries_scrape_batch_job_type', 'does_not_fall_back_to_scrape_shop_format') rather than user actions/outcomes per testing philosophy.
